themeWords.meaning: to press, oppress, or cause distress; to afflict
the personal name of the God of Israel; the LORD

sentenceTheme: Israel recalls repeated youth-long affliction yet trusts the righteous LORD to cut the cords of the wicked and render their plots fruitless.

verses.text: Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth, may Israel now say:

highlightedWords.meaning: to press, oppress, or cause distress; to afflict
Israel; the people/nation descended from Jacob

verses.text: Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth: yet they have not prevailed against me.

highlightedWords.meaning: to press, oppress, or cause distress; to afflict
to be able, to overcome, to prevail

verses.text: The plowers plowed upon my back: they made long their furrows.

highlightedWords.meaning: to cut in, engrave; to plow

verses.text: The LORD is righteous: he hath cut asunder the cords of the wicked.

highlightedWords.meaning: the personal name of the God of Israel; the LORD

verses.text: Let them all be confounded and turned back that hate Zion.

highlightedWords.meaning: Zion; the hill in Jerusalem, by extension Jerusalem

verses.text: Let them be as the grass upon the housetops, which withereth afore it groweth up:
Wherewith the mower filleth not his hand; nor he that bindeth sheaves his bosom.

highlightedWords.meaning: bosom, lap, fold of a garment

verses.text: Neither do they which go by say, The blessing of the LORD be upon you: we bless you in the name of the LORD.

highlightedWords.meaning: the personal name of the God of Israel; the LORD
